title: Lex vs Parse kind heuristic breaks if form parser gains position tracking
description: In parse_error.rs From<ParseError> for SieveError: the kind (Lex vs Parse) is determined by whether line.is_some(). The doc comment calls this an invariant: 'lexer always sets line/col; the form parser never does'. If the form parser is ever enhanced to track source positions (a natural improvement), all form-layer errors would suddenly be classified as Lex errors. There is no explicit discriminant in ParseError to distinguish source. MAINTENANCE CONSEQUENCE: the heuristic creates a silent misclassification trap for a routine future improvement.
